summary:
This extensive sync covered two major topics: IAS URL validation work and the Alpha Claw platform demo. Adam showed Brad a custom app he built to validate URLs from IAS Answer AI bot messages. By exporting all messages and checking unique URLs, he found only a 0.9% hallucination rate for bad URLs, providing hard data to counter IAS complaints about 404 errors. The hallucinations were mostly domain-level rather than path-level issues.

The bulk of the meeting was Brad demoing the Alpha Claw platform to Adam as a first-time user. Brad walked through the setup process including EC2 instance provisioning, Slack integration, dashboard features (gateway status, identity/soul configuration, cron jobs, integrations, memory, web app building, skills, and workspace files). Key features demonstrated included: automatic web app creation with Route 53 slug generation, voice notes via ElevenLabs, Slack search integration, secure credential management via AWS Secrets Manager, and the ability to build full Vite apps. They ran into issues with GitHub being down and Cameron having locked Brad out of Jira/Atlassian. Adam tested several features including Google Sheets access, logo generation, and budget tracking. They discussed future plans for Gmail read access, deploying apps to Netlify/Vercel, and the vision of Alpha Claw as a true AI coworker that can handle meeting transcripts, action items, and ongoing workflows.
keyDiscussionPoints: IAS 404 URL validation - 0.9% hallucination rate proven with data
Alpha Claw platform full demo and onboarding for Adam
Dashboard features: cron jobs, skills, integrations, web apps, memory
Slack bot routing - messages route to correct user's instance
Cameron locked Brad out of Jira/Atlassian - needs admin access restored
Security architecture with Lambda relay between Slack and OpenClaw
Future vision: Zoom transcript processing, Gmail integration
Alpha Claw as sellable service opportunity
decisions: Adam will use Alpha Claw and test it with real work
Brad to get Jira admin access restored (ask Cameron)
Gmail will start with read-only access for safety
Brad to set up Max on Alpha Claw next

actionItems.action: Use 0.9% hallucination rate data to respond to IAS 404 complaints
Set up Max on Alpha Claw
Fix thread response behavior in Slack integration
Push latest dashboard/onboarding updates to Adam's instance

followUp: Adam testing Alpha Claw with real work tasks
Brad restoring Jira access via Cameron
Thread behavior fixes in Slack bot
